verdict.summary: Round 2 resolves both prior major findings. The crate README now explicitly labels holder-side trust-expression evaluation as planned and states that no module, feature, or tests exist. The skill likewise confines implemented behavior to the existing framework-vocabulary layer and clearly says parsing, rewrite, evaluation, provenance assembly, fail-closed evaluation, and verifier re-check are not runnable claims until sq-6syab.4 lands with real-path tests. These statements are consistent with the existing framework_vocab surface, its vocabulary test, and research/trust-expression-spec.md, which schedules expression.rs and its acceptance tests as future work. No hostile reviewer-directed instruction appears in the diff.
